    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:11608:            "description": "Defines commands and events for browser extensions.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:11912:                            "description": "Allows callers to disable the promise rejection delay that would\nnormally happen, if this is unimportant to what's being tested.\n(step 4 of https://fedidcg.github.io/FedCM/#browser-api-rp-sign-in)",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:11986:            "description": "A domain for letting clients substitute browser's network layer with client code.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:12546:                    "description": "Encoding options for a screenshot.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:12578:                    "description": "Sends a BeginFrame to the target and returns when the frame was completed. Optionally captures a\nscreenshot from the resulting frame. Requires that the target was created with enabled\nBeginFrameControl. Designed for use with --run-all-compositor-stages-before-draw, see also\nhttps://goo.gle/chrome-headless-rendering for more background.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:12594:                            "description": "Whether updates should not be committed and drawn onto the display. False by default. If\ntrue, only side effects of the BeginFrame will be run, such as layout and animations, but\nany visual updates may not be visible on the display or in screenshots.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:12599:                            "name": "screenshot",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:12600:                            "description": "If set, a screenshot of the frame will be captured and returned in the response. Otherwise,\nno screenshot will be captured. Note that capturing a screenshot can fail, for example,\nduring renderer initialization. In such a case, no screenshot data will be returned.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:12612:                            "name": "screenshotData",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:12613:                            "description": "Base64-encoded image data of the screenshot, if one was requested and successfully taken. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:14767:                    "description": "Send events as a list, allowing them to be batched on the browser for less\ncongestion. If batched, events must ALWAYS be in chronological order.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:15039:                    "description": "Retrieve native memory allocations profile\ncollected since browser process startup.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:15147:                    "description": "The underlying connection technology that the browser is supposedly using.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:15815:                            "description": "Resource mimeType as determined by the browser.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:15820:                            "description": "Resource charset as determined by the browser (if applicable).",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:16105:                            "description": "The site of the top-level URL the browser was visiting at the start\nof the request to the endpoint that set the cookie.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:17641:                    "description": "Tells whether clearing browser cache is supported.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:17646:                            "description": "True if browser cache can be cleared.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:17653:                    "description": "Tells whether clearing browser cookies is supported.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:17658:                            "description": "True if browser cookies can be cleared.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:17677:                    "description": "Clears browser cache."
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:17681:                    "description": "Clears browser cookies."
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:17739:                    "description": "Deletes browser cookies with matching name and url or domain/path/partitionKey pair.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:17955:                    "description": "Returns all browser cookies. Depending on the backend support, will return detailed cookie\ninformation in the `cookies` field.\nDeprecated. Use Storage.getCookies instead.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:17991:                    "description": "Returns all browser cookies for the current URL. Depending on the backend support, will return\ndetailed cookie information in the `cookies` field.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:20835:                    "name": "screenshotRequested",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:20836:                    "description": "Fired when user asks to capture screenshot of some area on the page.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:20875:            "description": "This domain allows interacting with the browser to control PWAs.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:20880:                    "description": "The following types are the replica of\nhttps://crsrc.org/c/chrome/browser/web_applications/proto/web_app_os_integration_state.proto;drc=9910d3be894c8f142c977ba1023f30a656bc13fc;l=67",

    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:21040:                    "description": "Changes user settings of the web app identified by its manifestId. If the\napp was not installed, this command returns an error. Unset parameters will\nbe ignored; unrecognized values will cause an error.\n\nUnlike the ones defined in the manifest files of the web apps, these\nsettings are provided by the browser and controlled by the users, they\nimpact the way the browser handling the web apps.\n\nSee the comment of each parameter.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:21544:                            "description": "Frame document's mimeType as determined by the browser.",

    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:22462:                            "description": "The screenshots used by chromium.",

    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:22854:                            "description": "Capture the screenshot from the surface, rather than the view. Defaults to true.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:22861:                            "description": "Capture the screenshot beyond the viewport. Defaults to false.",

    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:24186:                    "description": "Fired when a navigation starts. This event is fired for both\nrenderer-initiated and browser-initiated navigations. For renderer-initiated\nnavigations, the event is fired after `frameRequestedNavigation`.\nNavigation may still be cancelled after the event is issued. Multiple events\ncan be fired for a single navigation, for example, when a same-document\nnavigation becomes a cross-document navigation (such as in the case of a\nframeset).",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:24416:                            "description": "True iff browser is capable showing or acting on the given dialog.     tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:28959:                            "description": "The command line string used to launch the browser. Will be the empty string if not\nsupported.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29019:                            "description": "List of types: https://source.chromium.org/chromium/chromium/src/+/main:content/browser/devtools/devtools_agent_host_impl.cc?ss=chromium&q=f:devtools%20-f:out%20%22::kTypeTab%5B%5D%22",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29062:                            "name": "browserContextId",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29098:                    "description": "The entries in TargetFilter are matched sequentially against targets and\nthe first entry that matches determines if the target is included or not,\ndepending on the value of `exclude` field in the entry.\nIf filter is not specified, the one assumed is\n[{type: \"browser\", exclude: true}, {type: \"tab\", exclude: true}, {}]\n(i.e. include everything but `browser` and `tab`).",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29169:                    "description": "Attaches to the browser target, only uses flat sessionId mode.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29199:                    "description": "Inject object to the target's main frame that provides a communication\nchannel with browser target.\n\nInjected object will be available as `window[bindingName]`.\n\nThe object has the following API:\n- `binding.send(json)` - a method to send messages over the remote debugging protocol\n- `binding.onmessage = json => handleMessage(json)` - a callback that will be called for the protocol notifications and command responses.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29258:                            "name": "browserContextId",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29266:                    "description": "Returns all browser contexts created with `Target.createBrowserContext` method.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29269:                            "name": "browserContextIds",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29270:                            "description": "An array of browser context ids.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29278:                            "description": "The id of the default browser context if available.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29327:                            "name": "browserContextId",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29328:                            "description": "The browser context to create the page in.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29368:                            "description": "If specified, the option is used to determine if the new target should\nbe focused or not. By default, the focus behavior depends on the\nvalue of the background field. For example, background=false and focus=false\nwill result in the target tab being opened but the browser window remain\nunchanged (if it was in the background, it will remain in the background)\nand background=false with focus=undefined will result in the window being focused.\nUsing background: true and focus: true is not supported and will result in an error.",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29406:                            "name": "browserContextId",
    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29724:            "description": "The Tethering domain defines methods and events for browser port binding.",

    tools/webapp-qa/node_modules/devtools-protocol/json/browser_protocol.json:29740:                    "description": "Request browser port unbinding.",

    <article className="workspace-stage-detail">
                  <div className="workspace-stage-detail__hero">
                    <div>
                      <p className="eyebrow">Stage scaffold</p>
                      <h2>{selectedStage.scaffoldTitle}</h2>
                      <p className="body-copy">{selectedStage.scaffoldSummary}</p>
                    </div>

                    <div className="workspace-stage-detail__badges">
                      <Badge tone={selectedStageStatus.tone}>
                        {selectedStageStatus.label}
                      </Badge>
                      <Badge tone={selectedStageAvailability.tone}>
                        {selectedStageAvailability.label}
                      </Badge>
                    </div>
                  </div>

                  <p className="workspace-stage-detail__note">{stageRoutingCopy}</p>

                  <CardGrid className="workspace-stage-detail__cards" columns={3}>
                    <SummaryPanel
                      description="Accepted detail, last-event summaries, and later live job progress can all drop into the same compact summary shell."
                      label="Current session signal"
                      title={buildStageDetailSummary(selectedStage)}
                    />

                    <SummaryPanel
                      description="Navigator links preview a stage through routing instead of mutating the backend-owned session snapshot in the browser."
                      label="Route mapping"
                      title={
                        <span className="workspace-stage-detail__route">
                          ?stage={selectedStage.stage}
                        </span>
                      }
                    />

                    <SummaryPanel
                      description={
                        selectedStageInvalidations.length > 0
                          ? selectedStageInvalidations.join(', ')
                          : 'Finalize sits at the end of the workflow and can remain review-only.'
                      }
                      label="Downstream impact"
                      title={
                        selectedStageInvalidations.length > 0
                          ? `Editing this step can refresh ${selectedStageInvalidations.length} later stage${selectedStageInvalidations.length === 1 ? '' : 's'}.`
                          : 'This terminal review step does not invalidate anything later.'
                      }
                    />
                  </CardGrid>

                  <SessionStageEditorPreview
                    invalidationLabels={selectedStageInvalidations}
                    selectedStage={selectedStage}
                    snapshot={snapshot}
                  />

                  <section className="workspace-stage-detail__list">
                    <div className="panel-heading">
                      <div>
                        <h3>Planned controls</h3>
                        <p>
                          These placeholder bullets mark the extension points for
                          the real business logic that will arrive in later prompts.
                        </p>
                      </div>
                    </div>

                    <ul>
                      {selectedStage.scaffoldBullets.map((bullet) => (
                        <li key={bullet}>{bullet}</li>
                      ))}
                    </ul>
                  </section>
                </article>
              </section>

              <section
                aria-label="Workspace overview"
                className="workspace-overview-grid"
              >
                <SummaryPanel label="Progress">
                  <ProgressBar
                    aria-label={`${snapshot.display_title} workflow progress`}
                    hint={`Resume at ${getWorkflowStageLabel(snapshot.resume_stage)} with ${progress.percent}% of the workflow currently complete.`}
                    label="Workflow progress"
                    value={progress.percent}
                    valueText={progress.label}
                  />
                </SummaryPanel>

                <SummaryPanel
                  description={buildPlanFocusCopy(snapshot)}
                  label="Story lane"
                  title={`${snapshot.selected_genre?.label ?? 'Genre pending'} / ${snapshot.selected_tone_profile?.label ?? 'Tone pending'}`}
                />

                <SummaryPanel
                  description={buildProductionCopy(snapshot)}
                  label="Production"
                  title={activeStage.label}
                />
              </section>
            </section>
          </div>
        </section>
      )
    }

    export function SessionWorkspacePage() {
      const { sessionId = 'unknown-session' } = useParams()

      return (
        <SessionWorkspaceProvider key={sessionId} sessionId={sessionId}>
          <SessionWorkspaceContent sessionId={sessionId} />
        </SessionWorkspaceProvider>
      )
    }

    Command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' frontend/src/shared/ui/buttonStyles.ts"
    Status: completed
    Exit code: 0
    Output:
    function cx(...classNames: Array<string | false | null | undefined>) {
      return classNames.filter(Boolean).join(' ')
    }

    export type ButtonTone = 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'ghost'
    export type ButtonSize = 'compact' | 'regular'

    type ButtonClassNameOptions = {
      className?: string
      fullWidth?: boolean
      size?: ButtonSize
      tone?: ButtonTone
    }

    export function getButtonClassName({
      className,
      fullWidth = false,
      size = 'regular',
      tone = 'primary',
    }: ButtonClassNameOptions = {}) {
      return cx(
        'button',
        `button--${tone}`,
        `button--${size}`,
        fullWidth && 'button--full-width',
        className,
      )
    }

      display: grid;

    .progress__meta {
      display: flex;

    .progress__track {

    }

    .progress__fill {
      display: block;
      height: 100%;
      border-radius: inherit;
      background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--color-gold), var(--color-brand));
    }

    .progress--moss .progress__fill {
      background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--color-moss), var(--color-brand));
    }

    .app-caption {
        justify-self: start;
      }

      .app-caption {
        max-width: none;
        text-align: left;
      }

      .app-utility-bar,
      .session-summary-grid,
      .session-card__meta,
      .workspace-shell,
      .workspace-stage-shell,
      .workspace-stage-detail__cards,
      .workspace-overview-grid,
      .workspace-topbar__status,
      .workspace-stage-grid,
      .card-grid--2,
      .card-grid--3,
      .form-columns,
      .sticky-summary-layout,
      .split-card {
        grid-template-columns: 1fr;
      }

      .workspace-topbar__actions {
        width: 100%;
        justify-items: stretch;
      }

      .split-card__aside {
        padding-left: 0;
        padding-top: var(--space-3);
        border-left: 0;
        border-top: 1px solid rgba(39, 69, 99, 0.08);
      }

      .workspace-pane--chat {
        position: static;
        max-height: none;
      }

      .workspace-chat-transcript {
        max-height: 26rem;
      }
    }

    @media (max-width: 640px) {
      .app-shell {
        padding-inline: 1rem;
      }

      .panel {
        padding: var(--space-5);
        border-radius: var(--radius-xl);
      }

      .app-nav {
        flex-wrap: wrap;
      }

      .panel__header,
      .session-group__header,
      .session-card__header,
      .session-card__title-row,
      .cta-row,
      .selection-card__header,
      .split-card__header,
      .workspace-stage-detail__hero,
      .workspace-chat-composer__header,
      .workspace-chat-composer__footer {
        flex-direction: column;
        align-items: stretch;
      }

      .toggle-field {
        align-items: start;
      }

      .selection-card__header {
        grid-template-columns: 1fr;
      }

      .selection-card__meta {
        justify-content: start;
      }
    }

    @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) {
      .panel {
        animation: rise-in 700ms ease both;
      }

      .panel:nth-child(2) {
        animation-delay: 90ms;
      }

      .panel:nth-child(3) {
        animation-delay: 180ms;
      }

      .panel:nth-child(4) {
        animation-delay: 270ms;
      }

      .app-shell__glow {
        animation: drift 16s ease-in-out infinite;
      }

      .app-shell__glow--right {
        animation-delay: -8s;
      }

      .loading-block {
        animation: shimmer 1.4s linear infinite;
      }
    }

    @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
      *,
      *::before,
      *::after {
        animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
        animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
        transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;

    Output:
    function renderRoute(initialEntry: string) {
      const router = createMemoryRouter(appRoutes, {
        initialEntries: [initialEntry],
      })

      return renderWithAppProviders(<RouterProvider router={router} />)
    }

    describe('app router', () => {
      afterEach(() => {
        vi.restoreAllMocks()
        vi.unstubAllGlobals()
      })

      it('renders the home screen inside the shared shell', async () => {
        mockBackendOnline()

        renderRoute('/')

        expect(screen.getByRole('link', { name: 'Sessions' })).toHaveAttribute(
          'href',
          '/',
        )
        expect(
          await screen.findByRole('heading', {
            level: 1,
            name: 'Pick up where bedtime left off.',
          }),
        ).toBeInTheDocument()
        expect(
          await screen.findByRole('link', {
            name: 'Resume Lanterns Over Juniper Lake',
          }),
        ).toHaveAttribute('href', '/sessions/juniper-lake')
        expect(await screen.findByText('Hello from FastAPI!')).toBeInTheDocument()
      })

      it('renders the session workspace route with a session id', async () => {
        mockBackendOnline()

        renderRoute('/sessions/moonlit-harbor')

        expect(
          await screen.findByRole('heading', {
            level: 1,
            name: 'Lanterns Over Juniper Lake',
          }),
        ).toBeInTheDocument()
        expect(screen.getByTestId('workspace-route')).toBeInTheDocument()
        expect(screen.getByRole('link', { name: 'Return home' })).toHaveAttribute(
          'href',
          '/',
        )
      })

      it('starts a new session from the home screen and routes into the workspace', async () => {
        mockBackendOnline({ sessions: [] })

        renderRoute('/')

        fireEvent.click(
          await screen.findByRole('button', { name: 'Start a new session' }),
        )

        expect(
          await screen.findByRole('heading', {
            level: 1,
            name: 'Fresh Session',
          }),
        ).toBeInTheDocument()
      })

      it('renders the not-found fallback for unknown routes', async () => {
        mockBackendOnline()

        renderRoute('/does-not-exist')

        expect(
          screen.getByRole('heading', { level: 1, name: 'Page not found' }),
        ).toBeInTheDocument()
        expect(screen.getByText('/does-not-exist')).toBeInTheDocument()
      })
    })

### Full Text

    Command: /bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba src/hooks/useBackendStatus.ts | sed -n '1,180p'"
    Status: completed
    Exit code: 0
    Output:
    1	import { useEffect, useEffectEvent, useRef, useState } from 'react'
         2	import { fetchBackendHello } from '../api/system.ts'
         3	
         4	export type BackendState = 'loading' | 'online' | 'offline'
         5	
         6	export type BackendStatus = {
         7	  checkedAt: string | null
         8	  isRefreshing: boolean
         9	  state: BackendState
        10	  label: string
        11	  detail: string
        12	  message: string
        13	}
        14	
        15	const loadingStatus: BackendStatus = {
        16	  checkedAt: null,
        17	  isRefreshing: true,
        18	  state: 'loading',
        19	  label: 'Checking',
        20	  detail: 'Checking whether the local FastAPI backend is reachable.',
        21	  message: 'Checking /api/hello...',
        22	}
        23	
        24	export function useBackendStatus() {
        25	  const [status, setStatus] = useState<BackendStatus>(loadingStatus)
        26	  const isMountedRef = useRef(true)
        27	  const refreshStatus = useEffectEvent(async () => {
        28	    setStatus((currentStatus) =>
        29	      currentStatus.state === 'loading'
        30	        ? currentStatus
        31	        : {
        32	            ...currentStatus,
        33	            isRefreshing: true,
        34	          },
        35	    )
        36	
        37	    try {
        38	      const payload = await fetchBackendHello()
        39	
        40	      if (!isMountedRef.current) {
        41	        return
        42	      }
        43	
        44	      setStatus({
        45	        checkedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
        46	        isRefreshing: false,
        47	        state: 'online',
        48	        label: 'Online',
        49	        detail:
        50	          'The app shell is connected to FastAPI through the Vite development proxy.',
        51	        message: payload.message ?? 'Backend responded without a greeting.',
        52	      })
        53	    } catch (error) {
        54	      if (!isMountedRef.current) {
        55	        return
        56	      }
        57	
        58	      setStatus({
        59	        checkedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
        60	        isRefreshing: false,
        61	        state: 'offline',
        62	        label: 'Offline',
        63	        detail:
        64	          'The shell still renders without FastAPI so frontend work can continue in isolation.',
        65	        message: 'Running in frontend-only mode.',
        66	      })
        67	
        68	      if (import.meta.env.DEV && import.meta.env.MODE !== 'test') {
        69	        console.warn('Backend status check failed.', error)
        70	      }
        71	    }
        72	  })
        73	
        74	  useEffect(() => {
        75	    let intervalId: number | undefined
        76	
        77	    async function primeStatus() {
        78	      await refreshStatus()
        79	
        80	      if (!isMountedRef.current) {
        81	        return
        82	      }
        83	
        84	      intervalId = window.setInterval(() => {
        85	        void refreshStatus()
        86	      }, 30_000)
        87	    }
        88	
        89	    void primeStatus()
        90	
        91	    return () => {
        92	      isMountedRef.current = false
        93	
        94	      if (intervalId != null) {
        95	        window.clearInterval(intervalId)
        96	      }
        97	    }
        98	  }, [])
        99	
       100	  return {
       101	    refresh: () => refreshStatus(),
       102	    status,
       103	  }
       104	}

### Full Text

    Command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '20,120p' src/hooks/useBackendStatus.ts"
    Status: completed
    Exit code: 0
    Output:
    detail: 'Checking whether the local FastAPI backend is reachable.',
      message: 'Checking /api/hello...',
    }

    export function useBackendStatus() {
      const [status, setStatus] = useState<BackendStatus>(loadingStatus)
      const isMountedRef = useRef(true)
      const refreshStatus = useCallback(async () => {
        setStatus((currentStatus) =>
          currentStatus.state === 'loading'
            ? currentStatus
            : {
                ...currentStatus,
                isRefreshing: true,
              },
        )

        try {
          const payload = await fetchBackendHello()

          if (!isMountedRef.current) {
            return
          }

          setStatus({
            checkedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
            isRefreshing: false,
            state: 'online',
            label: 'Online',
            detail:
              'The app shell is connected to FastAPI through the Vite development proxy.',
            message: payload.message ?? 'Backend responded without a greeting.',
          })
        } catch (error) {
          if (!isMountedRef.current) {
            return
          }

          setStatus({
            checkedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
            isRefreshing: false,
            state: 'offline',
            label: 'Offline',
            detail:
              'The shell still renders without FastAPI so frontend work can continue in isolation.',
            message: 'Running in frontend-only mode.',
          })

          if (import.meta.env.DEV && import.meta.env.MODE !== 'test') {
            console.warn('Backend status check failed.', error)
          }
        }
      }, [refreshStatus])

      useEffect(() => {
        let intervalId: number | undefined

        async function primeStatus() {
          await refreshStatus()

          if (!isMountedRef.current) {
            return
          }

          intervalId = window.setInterval(() => {
            void refreshStatus()
          }, 30_000)
        }

        void primeStatus()

        return () => {
          isMountedRef.current = false

          if (intervalId != null) {
            window.clearInterval(intervalId)
          }
        }
      }, [])

      return {
        refresh: () => refreshStatus(),
        status,
      }
    }

### Full Text

    Command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' tools/webapp-qa/scripts/run-spec.mjs"
    Status: completed
    Exit code: 0
    Output:
    import fs from "node:fs/promises";
    import path from "node:path";
    import process from "node:process";

    import puppeteer from "puppeteer";

    function parseArgs(argv) {
      const args = {};

      for (let index = 0; index < argv.length; index += 1) {
        const token = argv[index];

        if (!token.startsWith("--")) {
          continue;
        }

        const key = token.slice(2);
        const value = argv[index + 1];
        args[key] = value;
        index += 1;
      }

      return args;
    }

    async function loadSpec(specPath) {
      const resolvedPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), specPath);
      const rawSpec = await fs.readFile(resolvedPath, "utf8");
      const spec = JSON.parse(rawSpec);
      return {
        resolvedPath,
        spec,
      };
    }

    async function ensureParentDirectory(filePath) {
      await fs.mkdir(path.dirname(filePath), { recursive: true });
    }

    async function saveFailureScreenshot(
      page,
      outputPath = "../../.artifacts/webapp-qa/failure.png",
    ) {
      const resolvedOutputPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), outputPath);
      await ensureParentDirectory(resolvedOutputPath);
      await page.screenshot({
        path: resolvedOutputPath,
        fullPage: true,
      });
      console.error(`Saved failure screenshot to ${resolvedOutputPath}`);
    }

    async function waitForText(page, text, timeout) {
      await page.waitForFunction(
        (expectedText) => document.body?.innerText.includes(expectedText),
        { timeout },
        text,
      );
    }

    async function assertText(page, text, timeout) {
      try {
        await waitForText(page, text, timeout);
      } catch (error) {
        const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body?.innerText || "");
        throw new Error(
          `Expected text not found: "${text}"\nCurrent page text:\n${bodyText}`,
          { cause: error },
        );
      }
    }

    async function runStep(page, step, defaultTimeout, fallbackOutputPath) {
      const timeout = step.timeout ?? defaultTimeout;

      switch (step.action) {
        case "goto":
          await page.goto(step.url, {
            waitUntil: step.waitUntil ?? "networkidle2",
            timeout,
          });
          return;

        case "waitForSelector":
          await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {
            timeout,
            visible: step.visible ?? true,
          });
          return;

        case "waitForText":
          await waitForText(page, step.text, timeout);
          return;

        case "click":
          await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {
            timeout,
            visible: true,
          });
          await page.click(step.selector);
          return;

        case "type":
          await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {
            timeout,
            visible: true,
          });
          await page.click(step.selector, { clickCount: 3 });
          if (step.clear ?? true) {
            await page.keyboard.press("Backspace");
          }
          await page.type(step.selector, step.text, {
            delay: step.delay ?? 20,
          });
          return;

        case "press":
          await page.keyboard.press(step.key);
          return;

        case "hover":
          await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {
            timeout,
            visible: true,
          });
          await page.hover(step.selector);
          return;

        case "assertSelector":
          await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {
            timeout,
            visible: step.visible ?? true,
          });
          return;

        case "assertText":
          await assertText(page, step.text, timeout);
          return;

        case "assertUrlIncludes":
          await page.waitForFunction(
            (expectedFragment) => window.location.href.includes(expectedFragment),
            { timeout },
            step.value,
          );
          return;

        case "waitForTimeout":
          await new Promise((resolve) => {
            setTimeout(resolve, step.timeout ?? 500);
          });
          return;

        case "screenshot": {
          const outputPath = path.resolve(
            process.cwd(),
            step.outputPath ?? fallbackOutputPath,
          );
          await ensureParentDirectory(outputPath);
          await page.screenshot({
            path: outputPath,
            fullPage: step.fullPage ?? true,
          });
          console.log(`Saved screenshot to ${outputPath}`);
          return;
        }

        default:
          throw new Error(`Unsupported step action: ${step.action}`);
      }
    }

    async function main() {
      const args = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2));

      if (!args.spec) {
        throw new Error("Usage: npm run check -- --spec <path-to-spec.json>");
      }

      const { resolvedPath, spec } = await loadSpec(args.spec);
      const launchOptions = {
        args: ["--no-sandbox", "--disable-setuid-sandbox"],
        headless: spec.headless ?? true,
      };

      if (process.env.PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH) {
        launchOptions.executablePath = process.env.PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH;
      }

      if (spec.launchArgs?.length) {
        launchOptions.args = [...launchOptions.args, ...spec.launchArgs];
      }

      const browser = await puppeteer.launch(launchOptions);

      try {
        const page = await browser.newPage();
        const viewport = spec.viewport ?? { width: 1440, height: 960 };
        const timeout = spec.timeout ?? 15000;
        const outputPath = spec.outputPath ?? "./.artifacts/webapp-qa/latest.png";

        await page.setViewport(viewport);
        page.setDefaultTimeout(timeout);
        page.on("console", (message) => {
          console.log(`[browser:${message.type()}] ${message.text()}`);
        });
        page.on("pageerror", (error) => {
          console.error(`[browser:pageerror] ${error.message}`);
        });
        page.on("requestfailed", (request) => {
          console.error(
            `[browser:requestfailed] ${request.method()} ${request.url()} ${request.failure()?.errorText || ""}`,
          );
        });

        if (spec.url) {
          await page.goto(spec.url, {
            waitUntil: spec.waitUntil ?? "networkidle2",
            timeout,
          });
        }

        for (const step of spec.steps ?? []) {
          await runStep(page, step, timeout, outputPath);
        }

        if (!spec.steps?.some((step) => step.action === "screenshot")) {
          const resolvedOutputPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), outputPath);
          await ensureParentDirectory(resolvedOutputPath);
          await page.screenshot({
            path: resolvedOutputPath,
            fullPage: true,
          });
          console.log(`Saved screenshot to ${resolvedOutputPath}`);
        }

        console.log(`Completed spec ${resolvedPath}`);
      } catch (error) {
        const pages = await browser.pages();
        const page = pages.at(-1);

### Full Text

    `docker compose run` turned out to be the wrong tool for offline verification because it eagerly recreated dependencies, including the backend I had just stopped. I’m switching to `docker exec` against the already-running browser container so the failure-mode screenshots reflect the actual downed-backend state.

## Agent Message

- Time: `2026-04-01T03:56:53-07:00`
- Prompt: `28 — Error, Loading, and Feedback States` (29/100)
- Task event: `317`
- Summary: Implemented the shared feedback layer for the frontend: reusable banners, skeletons, inline spinners, a real toast store/overlay, backend connection polling with manual refresh, workspace live-feed status messaging, and a dedicated works...

### Full Text

    Implemented the shared feedback layer for the frontend: reusable banners, skeletons, inline spinners, a real toast store/overlay, backend connection polling with manual refresh, workspace live-feed status messaging, and a dedicated workspace error boundary. The changes are centered in [feedback.tsx](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/frontend/src/shared/ui/feedback.tsx), [appShellStore.ts](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/frontend/src/state/appShellStore.ts), [AppShell.tsx](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/frontend/src/app/AppShell.tsx), [HomePage.tsx](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/frontend/src/pages/home/HomePage.tsx), [SessionWorkspacePage.tsx](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/frontend/src/pages/session/SessionWorkspacePage.tsx), and [SessionWorkspaceErrorBoundary.tsx](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/frontend/src/features/session/SessionWorkspaceErrorBoundary.tsx). I checkpointed the code changes in commit `ccf16b2`.

    Verification passed with `cd frontend && npm run test`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build`. I also captured and reviewed browser screenshots for normal desktop/mobile states and offline/error states under `.artifacts/webapp-qa/`. The required reviewer summary is written at [28-error-loading-and-feedback-states.summary.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/prompts/28-error-loading-and-feedback-states.summary.md). One environment gotcha surfaced during offline QA: after intentionally stopping the backend to verify failure states, restarting it exposed a local `secrets.yaml` schema issue unrelated to this frontend work; that limitation is documented in the summary.
